ES1! · CME · RTH PLAN · THURSDAY JUN 18, 2026 · UPDATED 05:28 ET (PRE-OPEN)

ES RTH Plan — Updated for Overnight Repair

Charts: TPO with ON levels (05:26) · 30-min RTH+ETH (05:28) · VX1! 30-min (05:28) · Prior plan baseline: spike base 7,497 / yClose 7,492 / yPOC 7,582
Last 7,562.25–7,563.50 (+70 / +0.93%)
ON range 7,491 → 7,563.50
ON value: VAH 7,563.50 · POC 7,556.25 · VAL 7,539.75
Entire ON value at/above yVAL 7,540.50
Spike base 7,497 RECLAIMED ✓ · ON single prints 7,533.75 / 7,528.75 open below
20-EMA 7,518.25 — +44 below price
VIX 18.50 ▼ (−3.92%) · ATR 106.75
▲ What the overnight did (vs last night's plan) — repair complete, now at the supply wall The ETH played the base case and then some. From the 7,492 close it held the single-print band, reclaimed the spike base 7,497, filled the entire FOMC down-leg void, and built a fresh, symmetric overnight balance docked right at yesterday's value lower edge: ON VAH 7,563.50 / ON POC 7,556.25 / ON VAL 7,539.75 (ON VAL sitting on yVAL 7,540.50). Price now presses the fib extend 7,565 at the underside of yesterday's value — yPOC 7,582 / yVAH 7,600.75 directly overhead. So the FOMC flush is fully unwound, but the bounce has stalled exactly under supply, leaving fresh ON single prints at 7,533.75 / 7,528.75 as the new speed zone below. The RTH question is no longer "does it repair" (done) but "does it accept back INTO yesterday's value past yPOC 7,582, or reject there and roll back to the ON single prints / 20-EMA." The −$2.5B sell MOC inventory still sits at yPOC/yVAH — that's the wall this open has to clear. New annotations: ATH label 7,694; fib extend 7,565 / 7,621.75; ON VAH/POC/VAL band; ON single prints 7,533.75 / 7,528.75.

01Top-Down — re-based at 7,562.25

a) Daily context

ReferenceValueDistanceStatus
20-EMA7,518.25+44RECLAIMED — yesterday's FOMC candle closed below it; the overnight V clawed back above and holds it
50-EMA7,390.25+172Rising support — far below; major dynamic floor
100-EMA7,233.00+329Deep support
ATR106.75—ON used ~72 pts; a directional RTH still has roughly ⅓ ATR of budget

Read: Yesterday's wide-range FOMC distribution candle (close 7,492 from a 7,605.75 high) has been overwhelmed by the overnight V — price is back +44 above the 20-EMA and inside the prior multi-day range. The full MA stack (50/100/200/300) is rising well below, so the larger uptrend never broke; the FOMC day now looks like a one-day shakeout. But it is unconfirmed until price closes back inside yesterday's value: a daily close above yPOC 7,582 → yVAH 7,600.75 formally ends the flush and re-opens the upper stack (7,621.75 → 7,632.25 → poor high 7,648.75 → ATH 7,694). A close back under ON VAL 7,539.75 / 20-EMA 7,518.25 would print a failed-reclaim wick — the bears' only decent outcome from here.

b) 30-Min — overnight flow & updated levels

Segment (Jun 17 18:25 → Jun 18 05:28)Action
18:25–22:00Repair drive: held the single-print band, reclaimed spike base 7,497 and the RTH single prints 7,505/7,513, pushed through the 20-EMA 7,518.25
22:00–03:00Void fill: one-time-framed up through 7,528 to fill the entire down-leg, tagging ON VAH 7,563.50; built balance at yesterday's value edge
03:00–05:28Coiling under supply: balancing 7,539.75–7,563.50, now 7,562.25 pressing the fib 7,565 with yPOC 7,582 overhead

c) Market Profile — overnight detail

MetricValueRead

ON VAH / POC / VAL7,563.50 / 7,556.25 / 7,539.75ON value docked AT yVAL 7,540.50 — the market repaired up to yesterday's value floor and paused under supply
Spike base 7,497RECLAIMED ✓The bear-spike close is rejected; polarity flipped back to support, now ~65 pts below price
RTH single prints 7,513.50 / 7,505.25FILLED overnightYesterday's flush ladders repaired; that band is tested, two-way structure now
ON single prints 7,533.75 / 7,528.75STILL OPENThe fresh untested air — the speed zone that fills fast if ON VAL 7,539.75 gives way
Open projection~7,545–7,565Opens at/above ON value high, under yPOC 7,582 — repair template but pressed against the sell-MOC supply wall
UPDATED STRUCTURE MAP (pre-open) 7,694 ── ATH 7,648.75 / 7,632.25 ── poor high + swing 7,621.75 ── fib extend 7,600.75 / 7,605.75 ── yVAH + yHigh — full-repair ceiling 7,591.50 ── line 7,578.25 / 7,582.00 ── yPOC — THE WALL (sell-MOC supply) 7,565.00 ── fib extend ◄ YOU ARE HERE (7,562.25) 7,556.25 ── ON POC 7,550.50 ── line 7,539.75 / 7,540.50 ── ON VAL + yVAL — polarity floor 7,533.75 / 7,528.75 ── ON single prints (OPEN) ░ air ░ 7,518.25 ── daily 20-EMA 7,513.50 / 7,505.25 ── RTH single prints (filled) 7,497 / 7,492 ── spike base + yClose 7,472 ── yLow → 7,448.50 green zone → 7,396.50 fib …

Profile narrative: Overnight did the healthiest thing a bear-spike close can do: it bought the capitulation low, repaired its own thin structure (filled the 7,505/7,513 ladders, reclaimed the 7,497 spike base), and built a fat, symmetric value distribution back up to yesterday's value floor. That is repair — but unlike a bull-spike ratification, this distribution parked under yesterday's value, not on top of it. The auction question for RTH is therefore two-sided: "can price accept above yPOC 7,582 through the −$2.5B sell-MOC supply." Acceptance confirms the FOMC was a one-day shakeout and opens 7,600.75/7,605.75. Rejection produces rotations back through the fresh ON single prints 7,533.75/7,528.75 to the 20-EMA 7,518.25 — a now well-structured buy zone, not a trapdoor (unless the flush re-arms below 7,497).

VIX/macro carry-over: VX1 18.50 (−3.92%), low 18.20 — vol kept falling through the repair, confirming the FOMC selloff was orderly rate-repricing, not fear. Macro: the hawkish hold (Warsh, split dots, hike priced by October) is now spent/priced; the marginal surprise skews to dovish Fedspeak / soft data (squeeze fuel) vs a hot print / hawkish follow-through (re-arms downside). Watch the 2-yr yield (4.21%). Confirm VIX sub-19 at the bell for the full repair template.

03Context Into the Thursday Open

DimensionReadingImplication
Overnight characterOne-sided repair: bought 7,492 capitulation, filled the void, built value to yVAL — then stalled under yPOCHealthiest follow-through to a bear-spike close — but the pause is UNDER supply, not on top of value
Open location~7,545–7,565 — at ON VAH / fib, under yPOC 7,582Opens at the decision level; first 30 min vs 7,565 / ON VAL 7,539.75 sets the day
ON inventoryNet long (V-buyers from 7,472–7,540; FOMC shorts from 7,540–7,580 squeezed)Some long inventory to correct on a yPOC rejection — fade tags, buy ON VAL dips
Structural repair statusSpike base 7,497 reclaimed ✓ · 7,505–7,513 filled ✓ · ON single prints 7,533.75/7,528.75 OPENSupport below is REAL down to 7,497; the new air pocket only matters under 7,539.75
Overhead supply−$2,505.8M sell MOC inventory parked at yPOC 7,582 / yVAH 7,600The wall — expect the heaviest selling exactly at the void-fill magnet; fade the first tag
Value relationshipsON value 7,539.75–7,563.50 docked AT yVAL, below yesterday's value 7,540–7,600Must accept > yPOC 7,582 to flip to higher value; stalling = relief-into-supply
ATR budget106.75; ON used ~72Full-repair RTH ≈ 7,582–7,600 reachable; failure ≈ 7,500–7,520 — both map to your levels
Event clockThursday, day after FOMC; catalyst spent/pricedOften a fade/digest day; watch the 2-yr yield (4.21%) & Fedspeak for the swing factor

04What-If Scenarios — RTH Thursday Jun 18 (updated)

G1 · ON-VAL Dip Buy → yPOC 7,582 FadeBULL base case★★★★ 30%
Open / contextOpens 7,550–7,565; early two-way trade corrects into ON VAL 7,539.75–7,545 (= yVAL) and gets bought.
NarrativeValue rebuilds overlapping at the edge; a push through fib 7,565 runs to the yPOC 7,582 magnet where the sell-MOC supply caps the first attempt. This is the void-fill completing — fade the first yPOC tag, don't assume continuation.
SetupLong 7,540–7,548 · Stop 7,531 (under ON single print 7,533.75) · T1 7,565 (+19) · T2 7,578 (+33) · T3 7,582 (+38) · R:R 1.5→4.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: first hour holds ≥ 7,539.75. Invalid: 30-min close < 7,533.75 → G4.
G2 · yPOC Acceptance → Full Reclaim to yVAH 7,600BULL trend★★★ 18%
Open / contextOpens > 7,565 and accepts above yPOC 7,582 (momentum / dovish Fedspeak); shallow dips only to 7,565/7,556.
NarrativeFOMC distribution formally rejected. One-time-frame up through 7,591 to yVAH 7,600.75 → yHigh 7,605.75; a signing-grade dovish headline extends toward 7,621.75/7,632.25.
SetupLong 7,582–7,586 on acceptance over yPOC (two 30-min closes > 7,582) · Stop 7,572 · T1 7,600.75 (+18) · T2 7,605.75 (+23) · T3 7,621.75 (+39) · R:R 1.5→4.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX < 18. Invalid: re-entry below 7,565 → look-above-and-fail, expect rotation to 7,556/7,540.
G3 · Rejection at the Wall → Range DayNEUTRAL fade★★★ 24%
Open / contextMorning tags 7,565–7,582 (yPOC) and rejects with a wick, but ON VAL 7,539.75 holds below.
NarrativeThursday bracket 7,539.75–7,582: fade the edges; the overnight bounce was relief into the sell-MOC supply. Two-sided digestion of the FOMC, constructive only while ON VAL holds.
SetupShort 7,578–7,582 stop 7,591 → T 7,565/7,556; Long 7,540–7,545 stop 7,531 → T 7,556/7,565 · R:R 1.6→4.5 per rotation
Confirm / invalidConfirm: two failed wall-breaks by midday. Invalid: 30-min close outside 7,528–7,582.
G4 · ON-VAL Failure → Single Prints / 20-EMABEAR rotation★★ 15%
Open / context30-min close below ON VAL 7,539.75 — the level set that flips the day; the bounce is fading.
NarrativeThe fresh ON single prints 7,533.75/7,528.75 fill fast, 20-EMA 7,518.25 tested; if those go, the RTH single-print band 7,505–7,513 is the responsive-buy magnet ahead of the spike base 7,497.
Setup(a) Scalp short 7,537 stop 7,546 → T 7,528/7,518. (b) Core: long 7,505–7,513 or 7,518–7,522 on the responsive turn · Stop 7,496/7,511 · T1 7,528 · T2 7,540 · T3 7,556 · R:R up to 5.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: orderly, VIX ≤ 19.5. Invalid for longs: 30-min close < 7,497 → G5.
G5 · Hawkish Re-Arm → Spike Base / yLow FillBEAR tail★★ 9%
Zone / contextHawkish Fedspeak / yields breaking higher breaks the spike base 7,497.
NarrativeThe overnight V fully retraces: 7,492 yClose fails, air pocket to yLow 7,472 then the green zone 7,448.50 (50-EMA 7,390 worst case) — day-after de-risking amplifies the move.
SetupShort 7,495 on loss of 7,497 · Stop 7,506 · T1 7,492 (+3) · T2 7,472 (+23) · T3 7,448 (+47) · R:R 1.0→4.0. Then responsive long 7,472–7,475 stop 7,464 if VIX < 21.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: headline + yields ripping. Invalid: reclaim 7,540.
G6 · Day-After-FOMC Chop-and-DriftTIME-BASED★ 4%
Zone / contextQuiet digestion, no fresh catalyst; price oscillates around ON POC 7,556.
NarrativeClassic day-after-event: low conviction, tight rotations around 7,540–7,565, value building inside the ON balance. A positioning grind, not a directional day — scalps only, size down.
SetupTrade the ON value edges only: long 7,540 / short 7,564, ~12-pt targets, tight stops · R:R ~1.5
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX flat sub-18.5, no expansion. Invalid: 30-min close outside 7,528–7,582 → G1/G3/G4.

05Probability Matrix — updated

RankScenarioDirectionProb★
1G1 ON-VAL dip buy → yPOC fadeBull-repair30%★★★★
2G3 Wall rejection → range 7,540–7,582Neutral24%★★★

CombinedBull 48%Neutral 28%Bear 24%
Base case (updated)Open near the fib 7,565 / ON VAH, an early rotation into 7,539.75–7,548 (ON VAL + yVAL) that gets bought, then an assault on yPOC 7,578–7,582. The wall holds the −$2.5B sell-MOC inventory — first tags reject more often than they break when the bounce stalled under value like this. Net: buy the ON-VAL dip, take profits generously into/at yPOC 7,582, and only trust the breakout on two 30-min closes above it. True downside (G5) needs a fresh hawkish catalyst; absent that, the floor is ON VAL → 20-EMA.

06Decision Map — updated

THURSDAY RTH OPEN ~7,550–7,565 (fib 7,565 at price · wall yPOC 7,578–7,582 · ON VAL 7,539.75)
│
├─► FIRST MOVE DOWN (rotation, net-long inventory to correct)
│     ├─ holds 7,539.75–7,556.25 (ON VAL/POC = yVAL)
│     │     └─ G1 LONG → 7,565 → WALL yPOC 7,578–7,582
│     │            ├─ rejected (sell-MOC supply) → G3 range fade 7,540↔7,582

│                   ├─ BUY the turn 7,505–7,513 / 7,518–22 → back to 7,540/7,556
│                   └─ 7,497 fails (headline) → G5 → 7,492 → yLow 7,472 → 7,448.50
│

             └─ dovish headline → 7,621.75 → 7,632.25 → poor high 7,648.75

Position size vs ATR — applies to all threeATR 106.75; ON used ~72. 55–65% size, ¼-size on the G2 yPOC fade-short scalp. The tape is genuinely two-sided — #1 long and #2 short are both tactical (buy ON VAL, fade yPOC); #3 is the disappointment-buy. The yPOC fade is a SCALP at the supply wall, NOT a swing short — cover into the ON balance. Hard rule: any hawkish Fedspeak / yields-up headline = flat longs first, reassess for G5.

09Structural Warnings — updated

⚠ The wall yPOC 7,578–7,582 — first RTH test todayThe overnight bounce stalled right under it. Second/first RTH tags into the −$2.5B sell-MOC inventory reject more often than they break on the first attempt — the break needs RTH volume and two 30-min closes above to be tradeable. Don't front-run it with full size; fade the first tag.
ℹ RTH single prints 7,505/7,513 FILLED — warning clearedThe overnight repaired yesterday's flush ladders and two-way traded 7,492–7,563. That zone is now legitimate support structure; the old "speed zone" caution between 7,505–7,540 is lifted down to the 20-EMA.
⚠ Fresh ON single prints 7,533.75 / 7,528.75 STILL OPENThe new untested air, left by the overnight balance. Irrelevant above 7,540 — but if ON VAL 7,539.75 gives way, they fill fast and drag price toward the 20-EMA 7,518. Structure stops outside them (7,531), never inside.
⚠ Repair stalled UNDER yesterday's value — two-sided, not buy-dips-onlyUnlike a bull-spike ratification, this distribution parked below yesterday's value. The edge is buy ON VAL / fade yPOC, not chase. Long bias only holds while 7,539.75 holds; below it the day skews G4/G5.
⚠ Hawkish Fed re-arm = the only real bear catalystPrice structure alone no longer supports a bear case above 7,497 — the G5 path requires a fresh hawkish headline / yields breaking higher. The hawkish hold is priced; the marginal surprise skews dovish. Watch the 2-yr yield (4.21%).
ℹ VIX gateVIX 18.50 and still falling = orderly. < 18.5 = repair regime (favor G1 long / G3 fade) · 18.5–20 = two-sided · > 20 = repair template suspended, G5 in play.

10Session Transition — Thursday close → Friday

> 7,605 (above yHigh)Full repair + breakout; FOMC fully rejectedRange-high regime: 7,621 → 7,632 → poor high 7,648 → ATH 7,694 in view
7,582–7,605Accepted back into yesterday's value above yPOCBull-repair confirmed; buy dips to yPOC 7,582 / 7,565
7,556–7,582 (under yPOC)Repair to value edge but yPOC cappedTwo-sided; yPOC is the pivot — fade tags, buy ON POC dips
7,539.75–7,556 (in ON value)Balanced under supply — digestionCoiling at the value edge; direction off Friday's open vs ON VAL
7,497–7,539.75 (lost ON VAL)Bounce faded back through the single prints / 20-EMATwo-sided/heavy; 7,518 must hold, ON single prints are resistance
< 7,497Repair failed; spike base lost into the closeBear re-arm: yLow 7,472 → 7,448.50 → fib 7,396.50 / 50-EMA

11Final Summary — RTH Thursday Jun 18 (updated 05:28 ET)

ItemCall
Macro biasBULL (intact) — MA stack rising; 20-EMA reclaimed overnight (+44); FOMC now looks like a one-day shakeout
Intermediate biasNEUTRAL / repairing into supply — needs acceptance > yPOC 7,582 to confirm; poor high 7,648 still overhead
Intraday biasTWO-SIDED — buy ON-VAL dips, fade the first yPOC tag; directional-long only above 7,582
Base caseOpen near fib 7,565 → rotation into ON VAL 7,539.75–7,548 bought → assault on yPOC 7,578–7,582 → first tag rejected by sell-MOC supply
Primary tradeLong 7,540–7,548, stop 7,531, targets 7,565 / 7,578 / 7,582 (G1)
Counter-tradeShort 7,578–7,582 (yPOC first-tag fade) with a rejection wick, stop 7,591 → 7,565 / 7,556 / 7,540 (G3)
Disappointment-buyLong 7,505–7,518 if the bounce fades through ON VAL, stop 7,496 → 7,528 / 7,540 / 7,556 (G4)
Key bull level7,582 yPOC — acceptance (2× closes) confirms full repair, unlocks 7,600.75 → 7,605.75 → 7,632
Key bear level7,539.75 ON VAL — close below fills ON single prints → 20-EMA 7,518 → 7,505/7,497; below 7,497 re-arms the flush to 7,472
Biggest riskRelief-into-supply: the overnight bounce exhausts at yPOC and rolls back through the fresh ON single prints; hawkish Fedspeak / yields would accelerate it (G5)
Day-type expectationON-VAL buy + yPOC fade (≈30%) · wall rejection range (≈24%) · yPOC acceptance to yVAH (≈18%) · ON-VAL failure (≈15%) · hawkish re-arm (≈9%) · chop (≈4%)
ES RTH Plan · UPDATED Thursday Jun 18, 2026 05:28 ET · Last 7,562.25 · ON: H 7,563.50 / L ~7,491 / VAH 7,563.50 / POC 7,556.25 / VAL 7,539.75 · Spike base 7,497 reclaimed · RTH single prints 7,505/7,513 filled · ON single prints 7,533.75/7,528.75 open · yPOC 7,582 / yVAH 7,600.75 / yVAL 7,540.50 · VIX 18.50 · FOMC flush repaired overnight; battle at yPOC supply · Not financial advice. Verify VIX and final ON extremes at the bell.
